chapter_context.summary: Opens a formal covenant lawsuit where God pleads with His people using the mountains as witnesses, reminding them of historical deliverances, defining true religion ('to do justly, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God'), and condemning dishonest scales and industrial fraud.

outline.text: The Covenant Lawsuit Initiated: God's Appeal to the Mountains and Recalling Redemption from Egypt and Balaam's Plot
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Covenant Lawsuit Initiated: God's Appeal to the Mountains and Recalling Redemption from Egypt and Balaam's Plot (6:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

outline.text: The Great Requirement: Not Millions of Rams or Rivers of Oil, but Doing Justly, Loving Mercy, and Walking Humbly with God
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Great Requirement: Not Millions of Rams or Rivers of Oil, but Doing Justly, Loving Mercy, and Walking Humbly with God (6:6-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

outline.text: Judgment on Commercial Dishonesty: Scant Measures, Wicked Balances, and the Ruinous Statutes of Omri and Ahab
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Judgment on Commercial Dishonesty: Scant Measures, Wicked Balances, and the Ruinous Statutes of Omri and Ahab (6:9-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

key_themes.title: True Worship as Ethical Integrity
key_themes.description: Micah clarifies that religious rituals are meaningless without ethical behavior in everyday commercial, legal, and social interactions.

key_themes.title: Messianic Hope and Bethlehem
key_themes.description: The book introduces the eternal ruler coming from Bethlehem, pointing to the ultimate messianic restoration and security of God's sheep.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and requirements in Chapter 6, what does it practically mean for you to walk humbly with God in your current season of life?
How does the final vision of a compassionate God casting our sins into the deep sea inspire your daily worship and forgiveness of others?

cultural_historical.title: The Southern Kingdom under Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah
cultural_historical.text: Micah's ministry spanned the reigns of three kings of Judah, representing a period of political tension with Assyria, extreme corruption of the elite classes in Jerusalem, and eventually the reforming reign of Hezekiah.
